# feat(ml): add MLEngine single-point entry for kailash-ml 2.0 (Phase 2 scaffold)

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `daeb4d88ea72` — feat(ml): add MLEngine single-point entry for kailash-ml 2.0 (Phase 2 scaffold)

**Body**:

Implements the construction surface of specs/ml-engines.md §2 with
deferred Phase 3-5 method bodies.

Construction (§2.1 MUST 1-3):
- Zero-arg construction succeeds and produces a working instance
- Owns composition of the six primitives (feature_store, registry,
  tracker, trainer, artifact_store, connection_manager) via DI slots
- Every override is honored as-is (no silent wrap): e2 = MLEngine(
  tracker=custom) leaves engine._tracker is custom
- Backend resolved at construction via _device.detect_backend();
  explicit accelerator= on missing backend raises
  AcceleratorUnavailableError (translated from BackendUnavailable)
- Default store URL lazily resolved to ~/.kailash_ml/ml.db with
  KAILASH_ML_STORE_URL env override (no filesystem touch at
  __init__ time per zero-side-effects contract)

Method surface (§2.1 MUST 5) — exactly the documented eight:
setup, compare, fit, predict, finalize, evaluate, register, serve.

Phase-gated method bodies:
- setup / compare / fit / finalize raise NotImplementedError with
  Phase 3 pointer (Trainable adapters + Lightning Trainer land there)
- predict / evaluate / register raise with Phase 4 pointer
  (inference path + ONNX export)
- serve raises with Phase 5 pointer (multi-channel serving)

Pre-phase-body validation (§2.3 error table) runs BEFORE the
NotImplementedError so that caller-shape errors surface as typed
exceptions in Phase 2 already:
- fit(family=..., trainable=...) both set -> ConflictingArgumentsError
- fit()/compare() before setup() -> EngineNotSetUpError
- setup() with unknown target column -> TargetNotFoundError
- register(format=...) outside {onnx, pickle, both} -> ValueError
- predict(channel=...) outside {direct, rest, mcp} -> ValueError
- serve(channels=[]) or unsupported channel -> ValueError

Typed exception hierarchy per §2.3: EngineNotSetUpError,
ConflictingArgumentsError, TargetNotFoundError, TargetInFeaturesError,
AcceleratorUnavailableError, TenantRequiredError, ModelNotFoundError,
OnnxExportError, SchemaDriftError. Each carries actionable attributes
(column, requested, detected, framework, cause, ...) per §2.3.

Phase 2 is strictly additive — no existing module under
kailash_ml.engines.* is modified. The 2.0 cut (Phase F) will
relocate legacy classes to kailash_ml.legacy.*.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
